explanation: The code declares an array to store up to three numbers and their corresponding first and last positions in an input sequence. It continuously takes numbers as input, updates the internal state based on the input numbers, and manages their positions and counts to maintain a limited sequence of numbers. The algorithm ensures that only certain numbers are retained based on their relative positions and differences, and prints the current state of the sequence and the lengths of certain segments after each input.
name: 2010-05-06_day2q1
subject: Day2Q1
summary: This project includes multiple C++ files that perform operations on sequences of numbers based on user input, specifically tracking their occurrences and positions.
